title: NNStudio Trust Update Package Manifest
description: Manifest schema for NNStudio Trust Update Packages (TUPs).  A TUP atomically adds new trust anchors, revokes existing ones, or does both.  @kb: TRUST-ARCHITECTURE.md §TUP

manifestVersion.description: Schema version.  Must be 1 for this release.

type.description: Must be TRUST_UPDATE.  Any other value causes TrustUpdateHandler to reject the package.

id.description: UUID v4 uniquely identifying this TUP.  Used for replay-attack prevention — each id may only be applied once per truststore.

version.description: Monotonically-increasing TUP version.  TrustUpdateHandler rejects a TUP whose version ≤ last applied TUP version.

issuedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this TUP was signed.  Replay guard: rejected if timestamp predates the last applied TUP or is more than 30 days in the future.

issuerSubject.description: X.500 distinguished name of the certificate that signed this TUP, e.g. CN=NNStudio Root CA, O=NNStudio Project.

description.description: Human-readable description of why this TUP was issued.  Shown to the user in the confirmation modal.

addCerts.description: List of PEM-encoded X.509 certificates to add to the trust store.  May be empty.

file.description: Relative path inside the TUP archive to the PEM certificate file.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the PEM file.

role.description: Truststore slot: 'root' for root CAs, 'intermediate' for intermediate CAs, 'crl' for CRL files.

subject.description: Optional human-readable subject DN for display.  Not used for validation.

revokeCerts.description: List of certificates to revoke (remove from trust store) by serial number.  May be empty.

serialNumber.description: Hex-encoded certificate serial number to revoke.

issuerSubject.description: X.500 DN of the CA that issued the certificate being revoked.

comment.description: Optional human-readable reason shown to the user.

selfRevocationGuard.description: When true (default), TrustUpdateHandler refuses to apply a TUP that would remove the certificate used to sign the TUP itself.  Set to false only for emergency root rollover packages; requires explicit user confirmation.

requireUserConfirmation.description: When true (default), the Studio always shows a confirmation modal before applying this TUP.  Must be true for all TUPs that revoke certificates.  Setting false is allowed only for addCerts-only TUPs issued by the NNStudio Root CA.

signature.description: Relative path within the TUP archive to the detached PKCS#7 (.p7s) signature covering the canonical JSON of this manifest and all cert files listed in addCerts.
